name: Christianity
funFact: With over 2 billion followers, Christianity is the world's largest religion, spread across every continent.

name: Islam
funFact: Muslims pray five times a day facing the Kaaba in Mecca, the holiest site in Islam.

name: Hinduism
funFact: Hinduism is often called the world's oldest living religion, with roots stretching back more than 4,000 years.

name: Buddhism
funFact: Buddhism has no single creator god; its goal is liberation (nirvana) from the cycle of suffering.

name: Judaism
funFact: The Torah is traditionally read from a hand-written scroll, and copying one by hand can take a scribe over a year.

name: Sikhism
funFact: Sikh places of worship (gurdwaras) serve free community meals called langar to anyone, regardless of faith.

name: Shinto
funFact: Shinto shrines are marked by torii gates, which symbolise the boundary between the everyday and the sacred.

name: Taoism
funFact: Taoism centers on living in harmony with the Tao, the natural order underlying the universe.

name: Jainism
funFact: Jainism teaches radical non-violence (ahimsa); devout Jains take great care to avoid harming even the smallest creatures.

name: Bahá'í Faith
funFact: The Bahá'í Faith teaches the essential unity of all major world religions and of humanity as one family.
